methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Voyant Photonics is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Chris Phare that has raised $23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 private companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector at the same stage—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, implies a valuation of $46M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation across the 4 same-sector, same-stage private comparables, yielding $1.5B with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Voyant's revenue is not on file. 

Triangulating these two applicable methods produces a range of $39M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $773M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression methodology yields a modest result anchored solely to capital raised, while peer-median valuation is sensitive to the composition and stage classification of the reference set. Key uncertainties include Voyant's revenue traction relative to peers and the sustainability of current frontier-chip company valuations.
